description: R1c operation-order vectors. Each vector's per-site `order` (OperationOrder {orderId,frameId,onSuccessPath,dominatesSuccessReturn}) + the routine's `scopeFrames` (ScopeFrame[]) were produced by the REAL al-sem L2 walker (indexWorkspace, pre-resolve → computeOperationOrder inside indexRoutines, INCLUDING the error-call source-range order post-pass). `order` is ABSENT (key omitted, never null) when al-sem left it undefined; `scopeFrames` is empty for TryFunction/no-body routines and a single root `block` frame for a present-but-empty body. orderIds may have GAPS (exit/error leaves consume an id) + ALIASES (a leaf with both ids, and the error-call post-pass copy). Branch frames carry branchAlwaysTerminates + branchMayFallThrough even when false; branchTerminatesBy only when always-terminating. Hand-authored invariants were asserted against the live order before emit. Rust loads this committed file and asserts parity.

vectors.name: a_arg_position_call_before_enclosing_op
vectors.description: a call in ARGUMENT position (a conditionLeaf of the enclosing record-op) gets a LOWER orderId than the record-op that encloses it: `Cust.SetRange("No.", Helper())` → Helper() orderId 0 (arg leaf), the SetRange record-op orderId 1. The arg call is in expression position → dominatesSuccessReturn=false; the root record-op → true.
invariants: arg-position Helper() call has orderId 0 (before its enclosing op)
enclosing SetRange record-op has orderId 1 (after the arg call)
arg-position call NEVER dominates success return (expression position)
root record-op dominates success return (first root statement)

vectors.name: a_exit_leaf_consumes_orderid_gap
vectors.description: `nextOrderId++` increments per LEAF including the (unprojected) exit leaf. FindSet orderId 0, the `exit` leaf consumes orderId 1 (not emitted), the continuation Modify gets orderId 2 → the EMITTED ids are [0, 2] with a GAP at 1. Do NOT renumber.
invariants: FindSet has orderId 0
continuation Modify has orderId 2 (the exit leaf consumed orderId 1 → GAP)
emitted orderIds have a GAP (no leaf carries orderId 1)

vectors.name: b_if_then_before_if_else_explicit
vectors.description: explicit if/else: the if-condition leaf walks in the PARENT (root) frame; an if-then frame is pushed BEFORE the if-else frame (deterministic frameId). then-arm fallthrough → branchAlwaysTerminates=false + branchMayFallThrough=true (no branchTerminatesBy); same for else.
invariants: root frame 0 is a block with parentFrameId -1
frame 1 is if-then (pushed before if-else)
frame 2 is if-else (pushed after if-then)
if-condition record-op (IsEmpty) is in the PARENT root frame 0
then-body Init is in the if-then frame 1
else-body Modify is in the if-else frame 2
fallthrough then-frame: branchAlwaysTerminates=false + branchMayFallThrough=true (no branchTerminatesBy)

vectors.name: b_no_if_else_frame_implicit_else
vectors.description: a single-arm `if` (no explicit else) pushes ONLY an if-then frame — there is NO if-else frame for the implicit else. Only frames 0 (root) and 1 (if-then) exist.
invariants: exactly two frames: root + if-then
no if-else frame exists (implicit else)
frame 1 is the if-then

vectors.name: b_case_branches_then_loop_frame_order
vectors.description: case branches push case-branch frames in node order (frame 1 = arm `1:` fallthrough, frame 2 = arm `2:` exit-terminating). A following `while` pushes its loop frame (frame 3) AFTER the case frames, AND after its own condition leaves (which walk in the root frame).
invariants: frame 1 is case-branch (arm 1, fallthrough)
frame 2 is case-branch (arm 2, exit-terminating)
frame 3 is the loop (pushed after the case-branch frames)
loop frame omits all branch fields (not a branch)
loop-body Modify is in the loop frame 3

vectors.name: b_repeat_no_extra_frame
vectors.description: a `repeat..until` loop pushes ONE loop frame; the synthetic block that wraps the body does NOT push its own frame (it reuses the loop frame via walkBlock). Only frames 0 (root) + 1 (loop) exist. The body record-op lives in the loop frame.
invariants: exactly two frames: root + loop (no synthetic block frame)
frame 1 is the loop
repeat body Modify lives in the loop frame 1
no block frame other than the root exists

vectors.name: c_exit_arm_op_on_success_path
vectors.description: an op in an EXIT-terminating then-arm IS on the success path (`onSuccessPath=true`) — exit is a normal return, so ops before the exit DO execute on a success path. The exit-arm frame is branchAlwaysTerminates=true + branchTerminatesBy=exit.
invariants: op in exit-terminating arm is ON the success path (exit = normal return)
the if-then frame is exit-terminating (alwaysTerminates + by=exit + no fallthrough)

vectors.name: c_error_arm_op_not_on_success_path
vectors.description: an op in an ERROR-terminating then-arm is NOT on the success path (`onSuccessPath=false`) — every path through it terminates via Error(). The error-arm frame is branchTerminatesBy=error. The continuation after the single-arm error guard stays on the success path (true) AND dominates (the guard is not a normal-return).
invariants: op (Modify) in error-terminating arm is NOT on the success path
the if-then frame is error-terminating (by=error)
continuation Insert after error-only guard IS on the success path
continuation Insert dominates (single-arm error guard is NOT a normal-return)

vectors.name: c_both_arms_terminate_continuation_unreachable
vectors.description: an `if` whose BOTH arms exit → the continuation after it is UNREACHABLE → `onSuccessPath=false`. Both arm frames are branchAlwaysTerminates=true + branchTerminatesBy=exit. The continuation op lands in the root frame but is unreachable.
invariants: continuation Modify after both-arms-exit is NOT on the success path (unreachable)
continuation Modify does NOT dominate (unreachable)
both if-arm frames are exit-terminating

vectors.name: c_bare_top_level_error_follows_ambient
vectors.description: a BARE top-level `Error()` leaf follows the AMBIENT onSuccessPath — at top-level that is `true` (it does NOT get forced to false). Its dominatesSuccessReturn is false (Error never dominates a normal return). The error-call op inherits the callsite's order via the post-pass.
invariants: bare top-level Error callsite follows ambient onSuccessPath = true
bare Error never dominates a normal return (dominatesSuccessReturn=false)
error-call op shares the Error callsite's order (post-pass alias)

vectors.name: d_before_vs_after_conditional_exit
vectors.description: a root-block op BEFORE any conditional exit dominates the success return (true); a root-block op AFTER `if Cond then exit` does NOT (the conditional exit is a normal-return-possible-before-here). The exit leaf consumes an orderId → GAP. The if-then exit frame is branchTerminatesBy=exit.
invariants: root op BEFORE conditional exit dominates (true)
root op AFTER `if flag then exit` does NOT dominate (false)
the if-then frame is exit-terminating

vectors.name: d_nested_and_leaf_never_dominate
vectors.description: ops in a branch arm, a loop body, or a case branch NEVER dominate (they are conditional); bare `exit`/`error` leaves never dominate (the exit IS the return, not an op before it). Only the root-block FindSet dominates.
invariants: root FindSet dominates (true)
branch-arm Modify does NOT dominate
loop-body Delete does NOT dominate
case-branch Insert does NOT dominate

vectors.name: d_other_wrapper_rhs_call_is_condition_leaf
vectors.description: an assignment `n := Compute()` at the root is an `other` CFN node whose RHS call is a CONDITION LEAF (expression position): the call gets dominatesSuccessReturn=false (conditionLeaves never dominate), onSuccessPath follows ambient. This pins the REAL al-sem value; the `other`-wrapper dominance-PROPAGATION rule (no reset to false) is a structural port requirement covered by the differential.
invariants: the RHS call (Compute, a conditionLeaf of the `other` node) does NOT dominate
the RHS call follows ambient onSuccessPath (true at root)
the RHS call is in the root frame 0

vectors.name: e_tryfunction_no_order_no_frames
vectors.description: a `[TryFunction]` routine → computeOperationOrder returns empty maps + empty frames → NO site carries `order` (field ABSENT, never null) AND the routine has NO scopeFrames.
invariants: there ARE sites (non-vacuous)
NO site carries order (all absent)
the routine has NO scopeFrames

vectors.name: f_empty_body_root_frame_exists
vectors.description: a present-but-empty body → ZERO ops/callsites but the root `block` frame (parentFrameId -1) STILL exists. scopeFrames is NOT omitted just because there are no operations.
invariants: no sites
exactly one root block frame exists (parentFrameId -1)
the root frame omits all branch fields

vectors.name: g_error_call_copies_callsite_order
vectors.description: `if Cond then Error()` inside a guard: the error-call OPERATION (not registered in byOperation) inherits its PAIRED Error callsite's FULL order verbatim via the source-range post-pass — same orderId, frameId, onSuccessPath, dominatesSuccessReturn. The error-arm frame is branchTerminatesBy=error; the callsite/op are onSuccessPath=false.
invariants: Error callsite is on a non-root error-arm frame, onSuccessPath=false
error-call op copies the Error callsite's FULL order verbatim (post-pass alias)
root FindSet dominates (before the conditional error guard)
